symptoms.symptom_name: Acute heavy vaginal haemorrhage with large clots (pad soaked ~every 20 min)
symptoms.character: heavy bleeding with large dark clots

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Locally advanced cervical carcinoma with tumour haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Friable exophytic cervical tumour is a classic source of acute heavy vaginal bleeding.
Rate of loss drives circulatory compromise — the immediate threat to life.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Locally advanced cervical carcinoma with tumour haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Advanced pelvic tumour and acute haemorrhage produce severe pelvic pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Right flank/loin pain radiating to the groin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unilateral loin-to-groin pain suggests ureteric obstruction from parametrial tumour extension.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lightheadedness / near-syncope with cold, clammy, sweaty skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient-reportable proxies for hypotension and sympathetic compensation in haemorrhagic shock.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Locally advanced cervical carcinoma with tumour haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Offensive discharge from a necrotic cervical tumour is a hallmark of advanced cervical malignancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Postcoital and intermenstrual bleeding over recent months

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Locally advanced cervical carcinoma with tumour haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postcoital bleeding is a cardinal alarm symptom of cervical neoplasia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Locally advanced cervical carcinoma with tumour haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional weight loss supports an advanced malignant process.
Objective decompensated shock — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers circulatory compromise from reported faintness/clamminess, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pallor, diaphoresis, thready peripheral pulses and prolonged capillary refill (4 s)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician end-organ perfusion signs of shock — not obtainable over chat.
Palpable CVA tenderness suggests renal capsular distension from obstruction — requires clinician palpation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Friable exophytic cervical mass on speculum inspection

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Locally advanced cervical carcinoma with tumour haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A visible friable exophytic cervical lesion is the anticipated in-person finding — the AI cannot perform a speculum examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Invasive carcinoma on cervical biopsy histology

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis and staging cannot be confirmed; definitive oncological treatment cannot be planned.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Locally advanced cervical carcinoma with tumour haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Histology is the confirmatory standard — never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Right hydronephrosis on renal tract ultrasound

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Unrecognised obstruction progresses to acute kidney injury.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging confirms obstruction — not obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Anaemia (low haemoglobin) on full blood count

investigation_details.expected_result: Low haemoglobin consistent with acute blood loss; group-and-hold/crossmatch for transfusion
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Degree of blood loss and transfusion need cannot be quantified.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Quantifies blood loss and guides transfusion — a hospital blood test, not a telehealth input.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Locally advanced cervical carcinoma with tumour haemorrhage and ureteric obstruction
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Foul discharge + postcoital/intermenstrual bleeding + weight loss + unilateral flank pain together strongly indicate advanced cervical malignancy — a sealed reasoning cluster, not an AI-facing conclusion.
Massive bleeding with patient-reportable faintness/clamminess plus measured hypotension/tachycardia and poor perfusion defines a life-threatening emergency mandating 000.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency in-hospital assessment (examination under anaesthesia, speculum, vaginal packing as needed)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Direct visualisation and haemostatic control of the bleeding cervix — an in-hospital procedure the AI cannot perform.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, group-and-hold and crossmatch
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify blood loss and prepare for transfusion in shock.
Confirm suspected hydronephrosis and guide urological decompression.
Confirm and stage the malignancy once the patient is haemodynamically stabilised.
Exclude pregnancy-related bleeding as part of the ED work-up in any patient of potential reproductive capacity.
